title: Net Sales & Gross Profit
event: Ortiz Co. reports gross sales of $200,000, sales returns and allowances of $8,000, and cost of goods sold of $120,000. Compute net sales, gross profit, and the gross profit ratio.

computationPaths.narration: Strip returns out of sales first, then take out the cost of the goods. What remains is gross profit — the markup the business actually kept.

memorize.body: Net sales − COGS = Gross profit. GP ratio = Gross profit ÷ NET sales.
The ratio divides by NET sales, not gross. Returns come out before anything else.

questions.prompt: Gross sales $200,000, returns $8,000, COGS $120,000. Gross profit = ?

distractors.feedback: You used gross sales. Returns come out first: (200,000 − 8,000) − 120,000 = 72,000.
